task: Repair the road fatality density explorer without product drift
authority: Project Architect feedback from supplied screenshots

wordmark.rule: Render the full product name as one continuous non-breaking wordmark with a Yoruba-capable system font. The initial Ọ̀ must not appear detached from nàSafe.

remove: Inspired by the verified OpenCrashMap visual reference.
retain: Developed by ETL GIS Consulting LLC.
attribution.rule: OpenCrashMap is an internal benchmark, not public-facing promotional copy.

data_visibility.rule: The map must visibly communicate the data immediately after load.
requirements: Render one human figure for every accepted simulated fatal record.
Position figures from their actual GeoJSON coordinates, then group only when the selected bin creates a true shared segment.
Show dynamic totals for displayed deaths, occupied bins, daytime records, and nighttime records.
Show a ranked visible-segment summary derived from the active filters.
Use subtle entrance and pulse animation while respecting prefers-reduced-motion.
Show a clear empty state when filters return zero records.

geoaware_constraints: Do not create, estimate, multiply, or interpolate deaths.
Accept only fatal records with provenance, simulated verification, positive coordinate accuracy, and captured timestamp.
Do not change the operations API or product passport.

acceptance_tests: The title reads visually as Ọ̀nàSafe Nigeria without a gap after Ọ̀.
No public-facing OpenCrashMap attribution remains in index.html.
The initial map renders visible figures and non-zero summary metrics when the dataset loads.
Changing period, time, or bin size updates figures, totals, and segment summary.
JavaScript syntax, static contract, API tests, and dependency audit pass.
